hypothesis: Increasing post-recovery yaw damping from 0.2 to 0.3 amplified the contact-driven reversal and added an unsettled transition. Retaining world-frame tracking while reducing this gain to 0.1 should make the yaw-to-zero release less aggressive.
expectedEffect: Retain the world-frame score and severity improvement while bringing right-exact maximum yaw overshoot below 1.0 rad/s and keeping every yaw transition settled, without changing recovery dynamics or no-fall regressions.
